product_overview: The Workspace module provides authenticated users with a UI to list, create, update, delete, restore, and open workspaces. It includes a soft-delete (trash) workflow with seeded example workspaces to support testing and common user flows.
core_goals: Require user authentication before accessing workspace features and redirect unauthenticated users to /login.
Provide full CRUD operations for workspaces (create, read/open, update, soft-delete) with clear dialogs and confirmations.
Support a trash/restore workflow with a 30-day soft-delete recovery window.
Seed predictable default workspaces (HasNodes, NoNodes, ToDelete, ToUpdate) to enable consistent testing scenarios.
Surface clear validation and API error feedback in dialogs and lists to guide user recovery.

features.name: User Login
features.description: Allow users to sign in with email and password and redirect authenticated users to the workspace list.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Enter email in the email input field -> Enter password in the password input field -> Click the Sign In button -> See redirect to /workspaces -> See list of workspace cards
Navigate to /login while already authenticated -> App redirects to /workspaces
Navigate to /login -> Enter invalid email or password in the input fields -> Click the Sign In button -> See authentication error message -> Remain on /login

features.name: Workspace List
features.description: Show the authenticated user's workspaces and the seeded default workspaces; ensure the Workspaces tab is selected to load the list.
user_flows: Navigate to /workspaces -> Click on Workspaces tab in top navigation to ensure workspaces are loaded -> See list of workspace cards including HasNodes, NoNodes, ToDelete, ToUpdate
Navigate to /workspaces as an unauthenticated user -> App redirects to /login
Navigate to /workspaces -> Click on Workspaces tab in top navigation -> See loading spinner -> See error message if /trpc/workspace.list fails

features.name: Create Workspace
features.description: Create a new workspace using the add button which opens a title entry dialog, then confirm to add the workspace to the list.
user_flows: Navigate to /workspaces -> Click the add new workspace button -> Dialog opens with a Title input field -> Enter a title for the new workspace -> Click Create button to submit -> New workspace card appears in the list

Navigate to /workspaces -> Click the add new workspace button -> Dialog opens with a Title input field -> Leave Title empty -> Click Create button -> See validation error 'Title is required' -> Dialog remains open

Navigate to /workspaces -> Click the add new workspace button -> Dialog opens with a Title input field -> Enter a title for the new workspace -> Click Create button -> See API error message if /trpc/workspace.create fails -> Dialog remains open

features.name: Update Workspace
features.description: Edit an existing workspace's title via the Edit button on its card and save changes through an edit dialog.
user_flows: Navigate to /workspaces -> Find the ToUpdate workspace card -> Click the Edit button on the card -> Edit dialog opens with current title pre-filled -> Modify the title -> Click Update button to save changes -> Workspace card reflects updated title

Navigate to /workspaces -> Find the ToUpdate workspace card -> Click the Edit button on the card -> Edit dialog opens with current title pre-filled -> Clear the title field -> Click Update button -> See validation error 'Title is required' -> Title remains unchanged

Navigate to /workspaces -> Find the ToUpdate workspace card -> Click the Edit button on the card -> Edit dialog opens with current title pre-filled -> Modify the title -> Click Update button -> See API error message if /trpc/workspace.update fails -> Edit dialog remains open

features.name: Delete Workspace
features.description: Soft-delete a workspace from the list using the Delete button; deleted items move to /workspaces/trash and are restorable within 30 days.
user_flows: Navigate to /workspaces -> Find the ToDelete workspace card -> Click the Delete button on the card -> Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog -> Workspace is removed from the main list -> Navigate to /workspaces/trash to verify it appears there
Navigate to /workspaces -> Find the ToDelete workspace card -> Click the Delete button on the card -> Confirmation dialog opens -> Click Cancel -> Workspace remains in the main list

Navigate to /workspaces -> Find the ToDelete workspace card -> Click the Delete button on the card -> Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog -> See API error message if /trpc/workspace.delete fails -> Workspace remains in the main list

features.name: Restore Workspace from Trash
features.description: Restore a soft-deleted workspace from /workspaces/trash back to the active workspaces list via a restore confirmation dialog.
user_flows: Navigate to /workspaces/trash -> Find the deleted workspace card in trash -> Click the restore icon button on the card -> Dialog opens to confirm restoration -> Click Restore to confirm -> Workspace disappears from trash and reappears in /workspaces

Navigate to /workspaces/trash -> Find the deleted workspace card in trash -> Click the restore icon button on the card -> Dialog opens to confirm restoration -> Click Cancel -> Workspace remains in trash

Navigate to /workspaces/trash -> Find the deleted workspace card in trash -> Click the restore icon button on the card -> Dialog opens to confirm restoration -> Click Restore to confirm -> See API error message if /trpc/workspace.restore fails -> Workspace remains in trash

features.name: Open Workspace
features.description: Open a workspace by clicking its card to view workspace details and the node sidebar; behavior differs for workspaces with nodes vs empty workspaces.
user_flows: Navigate to /workspaces -> Click on the HasNodes workspace card -> Route changes to /workspaces/<workspaceId> -> Sidebar shows RootNode in the node list
Navigate to /workspaces -> Click on the NoNodes workspace card -> Route changes to /workspaces/<workspaceId> -> See 'No nodes yet' message
Navigate to /workspaces -> Click on any workspace card -> Route attempts to change to /workspaces/<workspaceId> -> See error message if /trpc/workspace.getById fails
Navigate to /workspaces/<workspaceId> as an unauthenticated user -> App redirects to /login

tech_stack.framework: React + TanStack Router v1 + tRPC v11

tech_stack.other: Bun runtime, TanStack Query v5, Zustand v5, Better Auth

routes.description: Authentication page with sign-in and sign-up toggle. Redirects to /workspaces if already logged in.
Workspace list page with create, edit, delete, and open operations
Deleted workspaces list with restore functionality (30-day recovery window)
Individual workspace view showing node tree in sidebar and chat/notes panels

features.name: User Login
features.description: Sign in with email and password to access the app. Required before accessing any workspace feature.
user_interactions: Navigate to /login
Enter email in the email input field
Enter password in the password input field
Click the Sign In button
App redirects to /workspaces on success

features.name: Workspace List
features.description: View all workspaces. Default workspaces include HasNodes, NoNodes, ToDelete, ToUpdate. Click Workspaces tab after navigating to ensure workspaces load.
user_interactions: Navigate to /workspaces
Click on Workspaces tab in top navigation to ensure workspaces are loaded
See list of workspace cards

features.name: Create Workspace
features.description: Create a new workspace by clicking the add button, entering a title in a dialog, and confirming.
user_interactions: Click the add new workspace button
Dialog opens with a Title input field
Enter a title for the new workspace
Click Create button to submit
New workspace card appears in the list

features.name: Update Workspace
features.description: Edit an existing workspace title via the Edit button on its card. Use the ToUpdate workspace.
user_interactions: Find the ToUpdate workspace card
Click the Edit button on the card
Edit dialog opens with current title pre-filled
Modify the title
Click Update button to save changes
Workspace card reflects updated title

features.name: Delete Workspace
features.description: Delete a workspace using the Delete button on its card. Use ToDelete workspace. Deleted workspace moves to trash.
user_interactions: Find the ToDelete workspace card
Click the Delete button on the card
Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog
Workspace is removed from the main list
Navigate to /workspaces/trash to verify it appears there

features.name: Restore Workspace from Trash
features.description: Restore a deleted workspace from trash back to the active workspaces list.
user_interactions: Navigate to /workspaces/trash
Find the deleted workspace card in trash
Click the restore icon button on the card
Dialog opens to confirm restoration
Click Restore to confirm
Workspace disappears from trash and reappears in /workspaces

features.name: Open Workspace
features.description: Open a workspace by clicking its card. Route changes to /workspaces/<id>. HasNodes workspace shows RootNode in sidebar. NoNodes workspace shows No nodes yet.
user_interactions: Click on the HasNodes workspace card
Route changes to /workspaces/<workspaceId>
Sidebar shows RootNode in the node list
Navigating to NoNodes workspace shows No nodes yet message

known_limitations.issue: All workspace routes require authentication; unauthenticated access redirects to /login
known_limitations.impact: Each test must log in first before accessing workspace features

known_limitations.issue: Deleted workspaces have a 30-day soft-delete recovery window
known_limitations.impact: Workspaces permanently expire after 30 days and cannot be restored

known_limitations.issue: Tests that delete a workspace affect state for subsequent restore tests
known_limitations.impact: Restore tests depend on a prior delete completing successfully
